sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 1.5g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, that is a really good sign.
sections.subtitleText: 1.5g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, that is a really good sign.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 61.3g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: 61.3g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 22.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 22.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 47% daily value, iron at 29% daily value, potassium at 39% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 47% daily value, iron at 29% daily value, potassium at 39% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: cheap staple with real payoff because this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. fibre-forward category identity because legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre. one of the cheaper ways to buy useful nutrition because that matters for real diets, not just theory.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: can be rough on digestion for some people because practical tolerance still matters. can take planning or prep because that is a small but real friction cost. prep time can still be a barrier because dry legumes especially ask more from the cook.

closing.summary: Navy Beans has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
